text.title: AWS S3 Storage Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules every application of `AWS S3 Storage` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Block Public Access MUST be enabled at account level AND bucket level; opening public access requires explicit ADR + security-lead sign-off.

rationale.source: AWS S3 Block Public Access docs; CIS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Public S3 buckets are the most common data-leak class; account-level block is the defensible default.

rule.statement: Bucket default encryption MUST be SSE-KMS (customer-managed key) for production data; SSE-S3 acceptable only for low-sensitivity logs.

rationale.source: AWS S3 Encryption docs; HIPAA + SOC2 reqs

rule.rationale: SSE-KMS gives per-bucket key rotation + audit trail; SSE-S3 is opaque to auditors.

rule.statement: Buckets holding critical data MUST have versioning + MFA-delete on; permanent delete requires MFA token.
rule.rationale: Accidental + malicious deletes are the #1 data-loss vector; versioning + MFA-delete is the recovery floor.

rule.statement: Bucket policies MUST tier objects: hot (Standard) → IA after 30d → Glacier Deep Archive after 180d. Indefinite Standard storage forbidden for non-hot data.

rationale.source: AWS S3 Lifecycle docs; FinOps benchmark

rule.rationale: Untiered S3 spend is 40-80% above tiered; lifecycle is the cheapest cost optimisation.

rule.statement: Bucket policies MUST contain explicit deny for `aws:SecureTransport = false` (HTTP) and for unintended cross-account principals.

rationale.source: AWS S3 Security Best Practices; CIS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Without explicit deny, HTTP access + cross-account writes can slip through allow-list logic.

rule.statement: Buckets subject to WORM compliance (SEC 17a-4, HIPAA, FedRAMP) MUST have Object Lock in Compliance mode with retention period matching policy.
rule.rationale: Compliance retention requires immutability; governance-mode locks are reversible by admin and may fail audit.

rule.statement: Production data MUST be cross-region replicated to a DR account / region; replication metrics MUST be monitored + alarms set on lag > 15 min.
rule.rationale: Without replication, regional outage = data loss; lag-alarm catches replication breaks before audit.

rule.statement: S3 buckets holding sensitive data MUST have server access logging or CloudTrail data events on, with logs in a separate audit account.

rationale.source: AWS S3 Access Logging docs; CIS Benchmark

rule.rationale: Without access logs, forensics on read/write events is impossible; CloudTrail data events are the auditor-friendly surface.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
